title: Replace cond-with-guards-and-fallback into pattern-matched heads
prompt:
Given:

```
def classify(n) when is_integer(n) do
  cond do
    n < 0 -> :negative
    n == 0 -> :zero
    n > 0 and n < 10 -> :single_digit
    n >= 10 and n < 100 -> :double_digit
    true -> :large
  end
end
```

Rewrite as multi-clause function heads with guards, preserving the bounds exactly. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Classifier do ... end`.

must_contain: def classify(n) when is_integer(n) and n < 0
def classify(n) when is_integer(n) and n > 0 and n < 10
def classify(n) when is_integer(n) and n >= 10 and n < 100

tier_rationale: Five clauses including a literal-zero head and a preserved-guard catch-all. Tricky because the cond's `true ->` must become a final guarded clause that still protects against non-integer input.
